title: Commentary on Investments: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Portfolio Composition
Total investments stood at $1,128,520 (in thousands) as of March 31, 2026. Available-for-sale debt securities comprised the dominant holding category, accounting for approximately 97% of total assets ($1,10,212 thousand). These instruments provide flexibility to sell before maturity if liquidity needs arise.

Minor Holding Category and Change Drivers
Time deposits represented a secondary position at $2,285 (in thousands), reflecting a decline of -23.2% versus the previous quarter. Other investments remained stable at $356 (in thousands). The overall portfolio growth of 2.0% was driven primarily by increases in available-for-sale debt securities.

Maturity Structure and Liquidity Implications
The maturity distribution shows assets spread across multiple horizons, including buckets for <=1 year, >1-3 years, >3-5 years, >5-10 years, and >10 years. This diversified structure may support near-term liquidity flexibility while maintaining exposure to longer-dated yields.

Integrated Conclusion
The portfolio’s composition—dominated by available-for-sale debt securities with a matured spread across multiple time horizons—supports the credit union’s overall liquidity position through both predictable cash flows and retained saleability. Management will continue monitoring shifts in market rates that could affect fair value adjustments on these holdings.
